metadata.title: Lebanese Official GS Grade 12 Mathematics — English Track: General Structural Reference
metadata.description: Era-agnostic reference describing the structure, exercise types, phrasing conventions, and topic patterns of the Lebanese GS Grade 12 Mathematics baccalaureate exam (English track).

hard_constraints.note: These hold true across all exams without exception.

calculator_permission.type: Non-programmable; cannot store information or draw graphs
calculator_permission.exact_arabic: يسمح باستعمال آلة حاسبة غير قابلة للبرمجة او اختزان المعلومات او رسم البيانات

candidate_order_flexibility.description: Candidates may answer exercises in any order
candidate_order_flexibility.exact_arabic: يستطيع المرّشح الإجابة بالترتيب الذي يناسبه (دون الالتزام بترتيب المسائل الواردة في المسابقة)

number_of_exercises.note: In the choice format, all exercises carry equal weight (4 pts each) and the student crosses out the exercises they do not answer.

marks_per_exercise.function_analysis: 4–8 pts (heaviest exercise in compulsory format; equal weight in choice format)

topic_positions.exercise_I: MCQ (4 questions, 4 options each; topics drawn from across the syllabus)
topic_positions.exercise_II: Complex numbers in the plane (affix transformation, locus)
topic_positions.exercise_III: Probability (urns, conditional probability, random variable, expected value)
topic_positions.exercise_V_or_beyond: Function analysis — Part A (auxiliary function) + Part B (full curve study) in compulsory format; self-contained in choice format

sub_part_labeling.exercise_parts: Part A / Part B (/ Part C in older format) — capital letter

exercise_I_MCQ.format: Table with 4 rows; each row = one question + 4 proposed answers (a, b, c, d)
exercise_I_MCQ.instruction_phrase_canonical: In the table below, only one of the proposed answers to each question is correct. Write down the number of each question and give, with justification, the answer corresponding to it.
instruction_phrase_variants: In the table below, only one of the proposed answers to each question is correct. Write down the number of each question and give, with justification, the answer corresponding to it.
In the table below, only one among the proposed answers to each question is correct. Write the number of each question and give, with justification, the answer that corresponds to it.
In the table below, only one among the proposed answers to each question is correct. Write down the number of each question and give, with justification, the corresponding answer.
MCQ_topic_pool: Complex numbers (argument, modulus, algebraic form, exponential form)
Domain of definition of composed functions (ln, arccos, etc.)

exercise_complex_numbers.intro_phrase: The complex plane is referred to a direct orthonormal system (O; u, v). Consider the points A, M and M' with affixes zA = ..., zM = z and zM' = z' such that z' = ... with z ≠ ...
standard_subtask_sequence: Write z' in exponential form for a specific z
Write z in algebraic form given condition z' = z
Show OM' = AM/OM (ratio property)
Show M' moves on a circle (C) when M satisfies a condition; find center and radius
Show (OM) ∥ (M'A) or (OM) ⊥ (M'A)
Show MM' = OA when M is on circle (C)

setup_types: Bags containing bills of different denominations with a die
Two fair dice rolled simultaneously (X = maximum or given rule)
Cards in a box (blue/red with odd/even numbers)
Compound sequential experiment (draw from U1 then U2)
exercise_probability.event_naming_convention: Single capital letters: A, B, C, D, E, F, R (Red), W (White/Win), S (Same color), etc.
standard_subtask_sequence: Calculate P(A/B) — conditional probability
Verify that P(Event) = [given fraction]
Calculate total probability P(A) = P(A∩B) + P(A∩B̄)
Knowing that [condition], calculate the probability that [event]
Calculate P(A∩B)
Determine the probability distribution table of random variable X
Calculate E(X) (expected value)
If the game is repeated N times, estimate the number of [outcomes]
exercise_probability.conditional_phrasing: Knowing that ..., calculate the probability that ...
exercise_probability.same_color_phrasing: Knowing that the [selected balls / drawn items] have the same color, calculate the probability that ...

exercise_similitudes_transformations.intro_phrase: Let S be the direct plane similitude that maps A onto B and B onto C.
standard_subtask_sequence: Calculate the ratio k of S (or: Show that k = ... and α = ... are the ratio and angle of S)
Show that a named point is the center of S
Determine the image of a line or point under S
Show two lines are parallel / perpendicular after transformation
Determine S∘S or the composition S∘S'
Show the composition is a dilation (when angles cancel)
Write the complex form of S: z' = az + b
Determine the affix of the center W of S (solve zW = a·zW + b)
Show three points are collinear as a consequence

exercise_function_analysis.structure: Part A (auxiliary function g) + Part B (main function f with full curve study). In the choice format, the exercise is self-contained with no Part A/B split.
exercise_function_analysis.function_type: Always involves eˣ and/or ln(x); pure rational functions without a transcendental component have not appeared.
exercise_function_analysis.auxiliary_function_connection: Part A establishes the sign of g(x) or h(x); Part B opens with 'Verify that f'(x) = g(x)' or 'Verify that f'(x) = eˣ·g(x)'.

curve_study_sequence.description: The fixed sub-part sequence that appears inside the function/curve study portion (Part B, or the full exercise in the choice format). Steps always appear in this order when present; not all steps appear in every exam.

steps.label: Limits at domain boundaries
typical_phrases: Determine lim f(x) as x → +∞.
Determine lim f(x) as x → −∞.
Determine lim f(x) as x → 0⁺.
Determine lim f(x) and lim f(x)/x. Give a graphical interpretation.
Determine lim f(x) and deduce an asymptote to (C).

steps.label: Asymptotes (deduced from limits)
asymptote_types_observed: Oblique: y = ax + b (most common in function exercises)
typical_phrases: Deduce an asymptote to (C).
Show that the line (d) with equation y = x is an asymptote to (C).
Show that (d) is an asymptote to (C) at −∞.
Show that (d) is an asymptote to (C) at +∞.
Determine the asymptotes to (C).
Determine lim f(x) and show that line (d) with equation y = −x is an asymptote to (C).
Show that line (d') with equation y = −x + 2 is an asymptote to (C).
Show that (C) is included between (d) and (d').

steps.label: Center of symmetry (optional)
steps.frequency: Occasional — present when f is a logistic/sigmoid-type function with an inflection at a midpoint
typical_phrases: Show that point W(0; 1) is the center of symmetry of curve (C).
Prove that the curve (C) has a center of symmetry W(a, b).

steps.label: Derivative f'(x) and table of variations
typical_phrases: Calculate f'(x) and set up the table of variations of f.
Verify that f'(x) = ... and set up the table of variations of f.
Show that f'(x) = ... and set up the table of variations of the function f.
Verify that f'(x) = h(x) and set up the table of variations of f.
Verify that f'(x) = e^x·g(x) and set up the table of variations of the function f.
steps.sign_inequality_variant: For all real numbers x, prove that [a] ≤ f'(x) ≤ [b].

steps.label: Sign inequality / positivity proof (auxiliary to step 4)
steps.frequency: Common — when Part A established sign of h(x) or g(x) which equals f'(x)
typical_phrases: For all real numbers x, verify that h(x) ≥ 0.
Show that f(x) > 0 for all x.
Deduce that eˣ ≥ x + 1 for all x.

steps.label: Equation f(x) = 0: unique root + numerical verification
typical_phrases: Show that the equation f(x) = 0 has a unique root α, then verify that −0.7 < α < −0.6.
Prove that the equation f(x) = 0 has a unique root α, then verify that −0.7 < α < −0.6.
Show that the equation f(x) = 0 has, on ℝ, a unique root α. Verify that 0.7 < α < 0.8.
Show that the equation f(x) = 0 has two roots α and 0. Verify that −1.6 < α < −1.5.
Show that the equation f(x) = 0 has a unique root α. Verify that 1.6 < α < 1.7.
Show that the equation f(x) = 0 has a unique root α and that 0.6 < α < 0.7.

steps.label: Inflection point
typical_phrases: Show that (C) has an inflection point whose coordinates are to be determined.
Prove that the curve (C) has an inflection point W whose coordinates are to be determined.
Show that the curve (C) has an inflection point I with abscissa −2.
(C) has a point of inflection W(0, 2). Find an equation of (T), the tangent to (C) at the point W.

steps.label: Tangent at a special point
typical_phrases: Write an equation of (∆), the tangent to (C) at its inflection point.
Write an equation of (T), the tangent to (C) at the point E with abscissa 0.
Find an equation of (T), the tangent to (C) at the point W.
Let E be the point on (C) where the tangent (D) to (C) is parallel to the line (d). Determine the coordinates of E.

steps.label: Relative positions of (C) and an asymptote/line
typical_phrases: Study, according to the values of x, the relative positions of (C) and (d).
Discuss, according to the values of x, the relative positions of (C) and (d).
Show that (C) is below (d) for all x ∈ ]0; +∞[.
Verify that f(x) − x = ... and study the relative positions.
steps.sign_study_method: Factor f(x) − [asymptote expression]; study sign by interval using roots and variation table

steps.label: Draw the curve
typical_phrases: Draw (d) and (C).
Draw (d), (d') and (C).
Draw (d), (∆), (d') and (C).
Draw (d), (∆), (d) and (C).
Draw (T) and (C).

steps.label: Inverse function (optional)
steps.frequency: Common — present in majority of exams
typical_phrases: Show that f has an inverse function g whose domain of definition is to be determined.
Prove that f has, over [0, +∞[, an inverse function f⁻¹ and determine its domain of definition.
Let g be the inverse function of f, and denote by (G) the representative curve of g in the system O; i, j.
a- Draw (G).  b- Solve the inequality ln(g(x)) ≤ 0.
Determine the asymptotes to the representative curve (C') of g.
Write an equation of the line (T), the tangent to (C') at its center of symmetry.
Draw (C') and (T) in the same system as that of (C).

steps.label: Area calculation
typical_phrases: Calculate, in cm², the area of the region bounded by the curve (G), the line (d) and the x-axis.
Deduce the area of the region bounded by (C), (d) and the two lines with equations x = α and x = 0.
Calculate, in terms of α, the area of the region bounded by (C), (d) and the lines with equations x = α and x = e.
Show that A(α) = (1 + α − eα) units of area.
Show that the area of the region bounded by (C), (C') and the coordinates axes is equal to 4ln(2 + eλ) − 2λ² units of area.

canonical_forms: Consider the function f defined on ℝ as f(x) = ...
Consider the function f defined over ]0; +∞[ as f(x) = ...
Consider the function f defined over ]0, +∞[ as f(x) = ...
Let f be the function defined over ___ as f(x) = ...
Let f be the function defined on ℝ as f(x) = ...
Consider the two functions f and g defined over ]0; +∞[ as f(x) = ... and g(x) = ...

curve_introduction.canonical: Denote by (C) the representative curve of f in an orthonormal system (O; i, j).
variants: Denote by (C) its representative curve in an orthonormal system.
Denote by (C) its representative curve in an orthonormal system O; i, j.
Denote by (C) the representative curve of f in an orthonormal system O; i, j; graphical unit is 2 cm.
Denote by (C) the representative curve of f in an orthonormal system (O; i, j).

limits.with_ratio_graphical: Determine lim f(x) and lim f(x)/x. Give a graphical interpretation.
limits.deduce_asymptote: Determine lim f(x) and deduce an asymptote to (C).
limits.show_asymptote: Determine lim f(x) and show that line (d) with equation y = ... is an asymptote to (C).
limits.show_at_minus_inf: Determine lim f(x) and show that (d) is an asymptote to (C) at −∞.
limits.show_oblique_positive: Show that lim [f(x) − x] = 0, then (d): y = x is an asymptote to (C).

asymptotes.show_oblique: Show that the line (d) with equation y = ... is an asymptote to (C).
asymptotes.show_at_direction: Show that (d) is an asymptote to (C) at −∞.
asymptotes.show_at_plus_inf: Show that (d) is an asymptote to (C) at +∞.
asymptotes.show_between_two: Show that (C) is included between (d) and (d').
asymptotes.show_below: Show that (C) is below (d) for all x ∈ ]0; +∞[.

derivative_and_variations.calculate_and_table: Calculate f'(x) and set up the table of variations of f.
derivative_and_variations.verify_and_table: Verify that f'(x) = ... and set up the table of variations of f.
derivative_and_variations.show_and_table: Show that f'(x) = ... and set up the table of variations of the function f.
derivative_and_variations.just_table: Set up the table of variations of the function f.
derivative_and_variations.sign_bound: For all real numbers x, prove that [a] ≤ f'(x) ≤ [b].

inflection_point.standard: Show that (C) has an inflection point whose coordinates are to be determined.
inflection_point.with_W_label: Prove that the curve (C) has an inflection point W whose coordinates are to be determined.
inflection_point.given_point: (C) has a point of inflection W(0, 2). Find an equation of (T), the tangent to (C) at the point W.
inflection_point.with_abscissa: Show that the curve (C) has an inflection point I with abscissa −2.

tangent.write_at_point: Write an equation of (T), the tangent to (C) at ...
tangent.write_at_inflection: Write an equation of (∆), the tangent to (C) at its inflection point.
tangent.find_at_W: Find an equation of (T), the tangent to (C) at the point W.
tangent.parallel_to_line: Let E be the point on (C) where the tangent (D) to (C) is parallel to the line (d). Determine the coordinates of E.

relative_positions.study: Study, according to the values of x, the relative positions of (C) and (d).
relative_positions.discuss: Discuss, according to the values of x, the relative positions of (C) and (d).
relative_positions.verify_expression_first: Verify that f(x) − x = ... and [then study relative positions].
relative_positions.result_phrasing: (C) is above (d) / (C) is below (d) / (C) intersects (d) at point O

unique_root.show_then_verify: Show that the equation f(x) = 0 has a unique root α, then verify that a < α < b.
unique_root.prove_then_verify: Prove that the equation f(x) = 0 has a unique root α, then verify that −0.7 < α < −0.6.
unique_root.on_R: Show that the equation f(x) = 0 has, on ℝ, a unique root α. Verify that a < α < b.
unique_root.two_roots: Show that the equation f(x) = 0 has two roots α and 0. Verify that −1.6 < α < −1.5.
unique_root.unique_and_bounds: Show that the equation f(x) = 0 has a unique root α and that 0.6 < α < 0.7.
unique_root.numerical_check_method: Evaluate f at both endpoints; show product is negative (IVT) or signs differ

draw_curve.in_same_system: Draw (C') and (T) in the same system as that of (C).

inverse_function.show_existence: Show that f has an inverse function g whose domain of definition is to be determined.
inverse_function.prove_on_interval: Prove that f has, over [0, +∞[, an inverse function f⁻¹ and determine its domain of definition.
inverse_function.use_monotone_argument: f is continuous and strictly [increasing/decreasing] over [interval], then it has an inverse function f⁻¹.
inverse_function.let_g: Let g be the inverse function of f, and denote by (G) the representative curve of g in the system O; i, j.
inverse_function.draw_G: a- Draw (G).  b- Solve the inequality ...
inverse_function.asymptotes_of_C_prime: Determine the asymptotes to the representative curve (C') of g.
inverse_function.tangent_at_center: Write an equation of the line (T), the tangent to (C') at its center of symmetry.

area.calculate_in_cm2: Calculate, in cm², the area of the region bounded by ...
area.deduce: Deduce the area of the region bounded by ...
area.in_terms_of_alpha: Calculate, in terms of α, the area of the region bounded by (C), (d) and the lines with equations x = α and x = e.
area.show_area_formula: Show that A(α) = ... units of area.
area.deduce_from_symmetry: the line y = x divides the region ... into two regions of equal areas

differential_equation.verify_particular_solution: Verify that u(x) = ... is a particular solution of (E).
differential_equation.form_DE_for_z: Form the differential equation (E') satisfied by z.
differential_equation.solve_and_deduce_general: Solve (E') and deduce the general solution of (E).
differential_equation.find_particular_initial_value: Find the particular solution of (E) verifying y(0) = [value].
differential_equation.find_particular_through_point: Find the particular solution of (E) whose representative curve...passes through the point A(...) [with tangent parallel to x-axis].
differential_equation.substitution_intro: Let y = z + u where z is a function of x.
differential_equation.substitution_intro_2: Let y = z + f(x) with z ≠ 0.

center_of_symmetry.show_phrase: Show that point W(0; 1) is the center of symmetry of curve (C).
center_of_symmetry.verify_method: Verify that f(a) + f(2c − a) = 2b for the center (c, b); or show f(−x) + f(x) = 2k

space_geometry.system_intro: In the space referred to a direct orthonormal system O; i, j, k, consider...
space_geometry.show_equation_plane: Show that [equation] = 0 is an equation of (P).
space_geometry.show_equation_example: Show that 2x – y + 3 = 0 is an equation of (P).
space_geometry.projection_phrase: Determine the coordinates of H, the orthogonal projection of F on (d).
space_geometry.projection_phrase_onto_plane: Determine the coordinates of K, the orthogonal projection of A on (P).
space_geometry.parametric_phrase: Write a system of parametric equations of ...
space_geometry.volume_phrase: Calculate the volume of the tetrahedron ABCD.
space_geometry.tangent_circle: Show that line (d) is tangent to circle (C) at point F.
space_geometry.skew_lines: Show that (AB) and (D) are skew (not coplanar).
space_geometry.perp_bisector: Denote by (∆) the perpendicular bisector of [AB] in the plane (P).
space_geometry.perp_bisector_direction: Verify that V(2; 1; −1) is a direction vector of the line (∆).

conics.show_on_ellipse: Show that ... is a vertex of (E).
conics.verify_equation: Verify that x²/a² + y²/b² = 1 is an equation of (E).
conics.tangent_on_conic: Write an equation of the tangent (T) to (E) at L.
conics.directrix_phrase: Write an equation of (d), the directrix of (E) associated to F.
conics.parabola_vertex_focus: the parabola with vertex V(a, b) and focus F(c, d)
conics.show_common_tangent: Prove that (GL) is a common tangent to (P) and (H).
conics.eccentricity_phrase: the ellipse (E) with focus F, associated directrix (d) and eccentricity e = [value]

similitudes.intro_pattern: Let S be the direct plane similitude that maps A onto B and B onto C.
similitudes.intro_with_center: Denote by S the direct plane similitude with center I that transforms C onto B and D onto G.
similitudes.show_ratio_angle: Show that the ratio of S is equal to [k] and that [α] is an angle of S.
similitudes.determine_ratio_angle: Determine the ratio (scale factor) k and an angle α of S.
similitudes.show_center: Show that ... is the center of S.
similitudes.image_of_line: Determine the image of line (AC) under S.
similitudes.complex_form: Write the complex form of S.
similitudes.affix_center: Determine the affix of the center W of S.
similitudes.composition_nature: Determine the nature, the center and the ratio of h = S∘S'.
similitudes.show_collinear: Show that I, D and L are collinear.
similitudes.angle_of_rotation: Denote by R the rotation with center O and angle π/2.
similitudes.show_dilation: S∘S is a dilation. Find an angle of S∘S and calculate its ratio.

complex_plane.system_intro: The complex plane is referred to a direct orthonormal system (O; u, v).
complex_plane.point_affix: Consider the points A, M and M' with affixes zA = ..., zM = z and zM' = z' such that z' = ... with z ≠ ...
complex_plane.exponential_form: Write z' in exponential form when z = ...
complex_plane.algebraic_form: Write z in algebraic form in the case where z' = z.
complex_plane.locus_circle: Show that when M varies on ..., the point M' varies on a circle (C) whose center and radius are to be determined.
complex_plane.arg_condition_phrase: Show that arg(z') = arg(...) + 2kπ, k ∈ ℤ.
complex_plane.show_MM_prime: Deduce that if M belongs to (C), then MM' = OA.

mark_notation.format: Parenthetical after exercise Roman numeral: I- (2 points), II- (2.5 points), VI- (7 points)
mark_notation.sub_part_labeling: Numbered: 1), 2), 3)... then lettered: a-, b-, c-...
mark_notation.part_labeling: Part A / Part B / Part C (capital letter) for subdivisions of the function exercise

always_present.note: Present in every exam. Always the heaviest or one of the heavier exercises.
Present in every exam — at minimum as MCQ items; often as a full standalone exercise.

function_types_observed.note: The function in the analysis exercise always involves eˣ and/or ln(x). Pure rational functions without a transcendental component have not appeared.
function_types_observed.auxiliary_function_pattern: A simpler function g(x) or h(x) (e.g., g(x) = x + 2 + 2e⁻ˣ, h(x) = 1 + xeˣ, g(x) = (x+1)eˣ − 1) is introduced in Part A. Its sign is established. Then in Part B: 'Verify that f'(x) = g(x)' or 'Verify that f'(x) = eˣ·g(x)'.
